tolerance.notes: Per docs/GRIDS_API.md §4.1 primary check. The vertical family has no transcendental math — sigma levels are pure rationals, eta levels are a single multiply-add (ak/p0 + bk), z/theta/z_star levels are stored verbatim, and hybrid_sigma_theta blends levels linearly. Every binding should therefore reproduce the same canonical .esm bytes byte-for-byte. The ULP fallback of docs/GRIDS_API.md §4.2 is not expected to fire; relative tolerance is 0.0 (strict byte equality).

reference_binding_notes: Per docs/GRIDS_API.md §4.3 the formal reference binding for ULP ties is julia. These vertical fixtures were generated by the python binding because it landed first (dsc-g4u); since every field is a pure rational or a value the caller passed in verbatim, the choice of generator does not change the bytes. When the julia binding lands (dsc-79n) the reference_binding field here will be updated to `julia` and the committed .esm files re-emitted for paper-trail purposes only.
golden_dir_notes: The conformance golden is the same committed canonical fixture set used by discretizations/grids/vertical/. Duplicating them under golden/ would leave two sources of truth for the same declarative configs. Each conformance binding reads the .esm files under golden_dir, strips the binding-specific provenance fields (provenance.binding, provenance.binding_version), canonicalizes via sorted-key JSON, and compares byte-for-byte to its own to_esm() output with the same stripping applied.

fixtures.name: sigma_uniform_n16
fixtures.notes: Small fixture (per bead dsc-bbj): uniform sigma, nz=16. Smallest resolution that still exercises the uniform-sigma generator and the n_cells / n_vertices / n_edges derivation.

fixtures.name: sigma_uniform_n64
fixtures.notes: Realistic fixture (per bead dsc-bbj): uniform sigma, nz=64. Research-model-sized column; exercises the same math path as n16 at the `realistic` resolution the bead specifies.

fixtures.name: z_troposphere_l32
fixtures.notes: Realistic geometric-altitude profile, 32 layers, 0 m → 34 km. Exercises the `levels`-verbatim path for non-sigma coordinates.

fixtures.name: eta_hybrid_l12
fixtures.notes: CAM-style hybrid sigma-pressure eta profile, 12 layers. Exercises the synthesized-level path (sigma = ak/p0 + bk) where one multiply-add per interface might in principle diverge across bindings if either operand got promoted — the strict 0.0 tolerance forces that divergence to surface immediately.

fixtures.name: theta_isentropic_l10
fixtures.notes: Isentropic theta surfaces, 10 layers, 280 K → 380 K. Same `levels`-verbatim path as `z_troposphere_l32` but with theta semantics.
